data:
Search results for "LLVM IR SSA form explained simple example static single assignment":

Title: Understanding static single assignment forms
URL: https://blog.yossarian.net/2020/10/23/Understanding-static-single-assignment-forms
Content: This time, it’s **single static assignment** (or SSA) form, a common feature in the intermediate representations of optimizing compilers. Like the last one, SSA is a topic in compiler and IR design that I *mostly* understand but could benefit from some self-guided education on. In a **single-pass** compiler, (4) is monolithic: machine code is generated as the compiler walks the AST, with no revisiting of previously generated code. * Optimization potential: because machine code is generated in a single pass, it can’t be revisited for optimizations. A single-pass compiler will struggle to take advantage of this ABI-supplied optimization: it needs to emit a stack slot for each automatic variable as they’re visited, and cannot revisit its function prologue for erasure if all variables fit within the red zone. By way of example: the following (not actually very helpful) function is **not** in a valid SSA form with respect to the `flags` variable:.

Title: Single-Static Assignment Form and PHI
URL: https://mapping-high-level-constructs-to-llvm-ir.readthedocs.io/en/latest/control-structures/ssa-phi.html
Content: * Single-Static Assignment Form and PHI. define  i32  @max(i32  %a,  i32  %b)  {entry:  %retval  =  alloca  i32,  align  4  %0  =  icmp  sgt  i32  %a,  %b  br  i1  %0,  label  %btrue,  label  %bfalsebtrue:  ; preds = %2  store  i32  %a,  i32*  %retval,  align  4  br  label  %endbfalse:  ; preds = %2  store  i32  %b,  i32*  %retval,  align  4  br  label  %endend:  ; preds = %btrue, %bfalse  %1  =  load  i32,  i32*  %retval,  align  4  ret  i32  %1}. We can see that the function allocates space on the stack with `alloca` , where the bigger value is stored. In one branch `%a` is stored, while in the other branch `%b` is stored to the stack allocated memory. define  i32  @max(i32  %a,  i32  %b)  {entry:  %0  =  icmp  sgt  i32  %a,  %b  br  i1  %0,  label  %btrue,  label  %bfalsebtrue:  %retval  =  %a  br  label  %endbfalse:  %retval  =  %b  br  label  %endend:  ret  i32  %retval}. end:  %retval  =  phi  i32  [%a,  %btrue],  [%b,  %bfalse].

Title: Static single-assignment form - Wikipedia
URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Static_single-assignment_form
Content: SSA is a type of intermediate representation (IR) where each variable is assigned exactly once. SSA is used in most high-quality optimizing compilers.

Title: Methods to implement a register-based IR in static single ...
URL: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/8657557/methods-to-implement-a-register-based-ir-in-static-single-assignment-form
Content: You use LLVM to get a IR in SSA form so that you can do many dataflow analysis on this form. E.g. you convert C code to SSA IR using LLVM and

Title: precise construction of static single assignment programs using ...
URL: https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0164121220300704
Content: The Static Single Assignment (SSA) form is an intermediate representation used for the analysis and optimization of programs in modern compilers.
